chief_complaint: Generalized weakness and dizziness in elderly patient
clinical_reasoning: 79-year-old female presenting with generalized weakness, dizziness, and fatigue. Near-fall without injury. Vital signs are within normal limits: T 36.4°C, HR 78, BP 118/68, RR 20, SpO2 96%, AVPU Alert. No fever, no tachycardia, no hypotension by standard thresholds. qSOFA score 0/3, SIRS criteria 0/4. Patient is alert and oriented. The presentation is non-specific in an elderly patient — most consistent with dehydration, viral illness, or medication side effects. Immunosuppression noted but no overt signs of infection. YELLOW — urgent but stable, monitor closely.
